question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user invoke subscribe() with no authentication check present, causing the system to initiate gRPC topic subscriptions on behalf of the monitor without any signer or owner approval, effectively authorizing data-stream access to arbitrary topics?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of ownership validation in createSubscriptions() when iterating subscribeProperties.getGrpc().values(), causing GrpcSubscription objects to be created for topics the caller does not own or have signing authority over?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malicious expression into properties.getTopicId() that is then passed to expressionConverter.convert(), causing the converter to resolve a topic ID the attacker does not own, thereby authorizing a subscription without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that properties.setTopicId(topicId) mutates a shared GrpcSubscriberProperties object after expressionConverter.convert() resolves the topic, causing a race condition where a different subscription's topic ID is overwritten, authorizing access to a topic without the legitimate owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger clientSubscribe() without any authorization gate, causing grpcClient.subscribe(subscription) to be called with a GrpcSubscription that references a topic the caller has no signing authority over, effectively authorizing a transaction stream without owner consent?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit shouldRetry() which only blocks retries on INVALID_ARGUMENT but retries on UNAUTHENTICATED and PERMISSION_DENIED gRPC status codes, causing the system to repeatedly re-attempt unauthorized subscriptions and effectively brute-force authorization past transient auth failures?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where shouldRetry() returns true for a PERMISSION_DENIED error (since only INVALID_ARGUMENT is excluded), causing the retry backoff loop to continuously re-submit subscription requests to a topic owned by another party without that owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that shouldRetry() treats UNAUTHENTICATED as a retryable condition (not INVALID_ARGUMENT), causing the system to retry subscription attempts indefinitely with Long.MAX_VALUE maxAttempts, effectively authorizing repeated unauthorized access attempts without any owner intervention?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user call getSubscriptions() with no access control, obtaining the cached Flux<GrpcSubscription> that reveals topic IDs, subscriber counts, and subscription state of topics owned by other parties, enabling targeted unauthorized subscription attempts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache() pattern, where the cached subscriptions Flux is shared across all callers, causing a poisoned subscription (with an attacker-controlled topicId) to be replayed to all subsequent subscribers without re-validating owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Retry.backoff() configuration in clientSubscribe() with maxAttempts set to Long.MAX_VALUE (from GrpcSubscriberProperties constructor), causing the system to authorize and re-authorize subscription attempts to an unowned topic for an effectively unlimited number of retries?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a topicId value that, after expressionConverter.convert() processing, resolves to a topic ID belonging to another owner, with no post-conversion ownership check in createSubscriptions(), causing an unauthorized subscription to be authorized and cached?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger subscribe() which calls subscriptions.flatMap(this::clientSubscribe) with no per-subscription authorization check, causing all cached GrpcSubscription objects (potentially including topics owned by others) to be subscribed to simultaneously without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnError logging in clientSubscribe() which logs the full subscription object on error, causing sensitive topic ownership information to be leaked in logs, enabling the attacker to identify topics to target for unauthorized subscription?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the loop for (int i = 1; i <= properties.getSubscribers(); ++i) in createSubscriptions() with subscribers set to 1024 (the max), causing 1024 unauthorized subscription instances to be created for a single topic without the topic owner's approval, amplifying unauthorized access?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getStatusCode()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Throwable that is not a StatusRuntimeException, causing getStatusCode() to return Status.Code.UNKNOWN, which is not INVALID_ARGUMENT, causing shouldRetry() to return true and the system to retry an unauthorized subscription indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a signer key check in clientSubscribe() before calling grpcClient.subscribe(subscription), causing the system to submit a TopicMessageQuery for a topic that requires submit key authorization without providing or validating that key?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscribeProperties.isEnabled() and properties.isEnabled() are the only guards in createSubscriptions(), with no ownership or signer verification, causing subscriptions to be created for any configured topic regardless of whether the caller is an authorized signer?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive Flux pipeline in subscribe() where subscriptions.flatMap(this::clientSubscribe) processes all subscriptions concurrently with no per-item authorization check, causing multiple unauthorized topic subscriptions to be authorized in parallel without owner consent?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable shared state of GrpcSubscriberProperties where properties.setTopicId(topicId) is called on the same object used by all GrpcSubscription instances, causing a concurrent modification to redirect all subscriptions to an attacker-controlled topic without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry.getMaxAttempts() value of Long.MAX_VALUE set in GrpcSubscriberProperties constructor, combined with shouldRetry() passing UNAUTHENTICATED errors, to cause the system to authorize an effectively infinite number of subscription attempts to a topic without the owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the expressionConverter.convert(properties.getTopicId()) call with a specially crafted expression string that resolves to a topic ID owned by a high-value account, causing the system to authorize a subscription to that topic without the owner's signing key?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger subscribe() multiple times, and since subscriptions is a cached Flux, cause the same set of unauthorized subscriptions (including topics not owned by the caller) to be re-subscribed without re-evaluating ownership or signer authorization?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doBeforeRetry logging in clientSubscribe() which logs r.failure().getMessage(), causing sensitive error messages (potentially containing topic ownership or auth token details) to be exposed in logs, aiding further unauthorized subscription attempts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any cryptographic signature verification in createSubscriptions() when building GrpcSubscription objects, causing the system to authorize topic subscriptions that would normally require a submit key or admin key signature from the topic owner?
